title: Gedatolisib Combined with Palbociclib and Letrozole in Patients with No Prior Systemic Therapy for Hormone Receptor-Positive, HER2-Negative Advanced Breast Cancer.
abstract: Nonclinical evidence demonstrating that estrogen receptor, cyclin-dependent kinases 4 and 6 (CDK4/6), and PI3K/AKT/mTOR (PAM) pathways cross-promote tumor proliferation in hormone receptor-positive (HR+)/HER2- breast cancer cell lines led to the development of CDK4/6 inhibitors and agents inhibiting single PAM pathway nodes to treat HR+/HER2- advanced breast cancer. Simultaneous blockade of the estrogen receptor, CDK4/6, and PAM pathways may optimize antitumor control in the treatment-naïve advanced breast cancer setting. Gedatolisib, a pan-PI3K/mTOR inhibitor, was evaluated as first-line therapy, combined with standard-of-care palbociclib and letrozole, for patients with HR+/HER2- advanced breast cancer.
